# Benchmark Manifest

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate_collision_benchmark_manifest.py; do not edit by hand. -->

This manifest is generated from Google Benchmark JSON outputs and groups rows
by DART-owned feature/algorithm family. It intentionally reports only the
strongest comparison timing, not the comparison implementation name.

## Run Policy

- Generated: `2026-05-20T06:09:58-07:00`
- Generator: `scripts/generate_collision_benchmark_manifest.py`
- JSON sources: `.benchmark_results/collision_check_adapter.json`, `.benchmark_results/collision_check_distance.json`, `.benchmark_results/collision_check_mesh.json`, `.benchmark_results/collision_check_mixed.json`, `.benchmark_results/collision_check_narrow.json`, `.benchmark_results/collision_check_narrow_adapter.json`, `.benchmark_results/collision_check_narrow_raw_reference.json`, `.benchmark_results/collision_check_raycast.json`, `.benchmark_results/collision_check_raycast_batch.json`
- Metric: `cpu_time` median, with mean/stddev retained for variance context.
- Build type: `release`
- Host CPU: `32 x 5300 MHz`
- Benchmark worker threads: `1` per Google Benchmark row.
- CPU scaling enabled: `True`
- ASLR enabled: `False, True`
- Status caveat: this is a first inventory run; final acceptance evidence should rerun under quieter load and CPU policy.
